Grad Café Analytics

API Reference

Auto-generated from docstrings in module_4/src/.

Flask Application (app.py)

app.py — Flask web application for Grad Café SQL analysis.

Routes:

GET /analysis — main analysis page GET / — redirects to /analysis (backwards compat) POST /pull-data — start background scrape + DB load GET /pull-status — JSON: is a scrape currently running? POST /update-analysis — refresh analysis (rejects if scrape running)

Design note: create_app() is a factory function — it builds and returns a Flask app instead of creating one at module level. This lets tests call create_app({“TESTING”: True, “DATABASE_URL”: “…”}) to get a clean, isolated app pointed at a test database, without touching production state.

app.create_app(config: dict | None = None)[source]

Application factory — build and return a configured Flask app.

Parameters:

config (dict or None) – Optional dict of Flask config overrides. Tests use this to inject TESTING=True, DATABASE_URL, and a fake scraper callable so no real network or DB is touched.

Returns:

A fully configured Flask application instance.

Return type:

flask.Flask

Database Helper (db.py)

db.py — shared database connection helper for module_4.

Reads DATABASE_URL from the environment so that tests and CI can point at a throwaway database without touching this file.

Expected format: postgresql://user:password@host:port/dbname Homebrew default (no password, OS user): postgresql://localhost/gradcafe

db.get_conn(database_url: str | None = None)[source]

Return an open psycopg connection.

Parameters:

database_url (str or None) – Override the DATABASE_URL env var (used by tests).

Returns:

An open psycopg connection.

Return type:

psycopg.Connection

SQL Queries (query_data.py)

query_data.py — SQL analysis of the Grad Café applicants table.

Answers all required questions and two additional original questions. Run directly to print results to the console.

Usage:

python query_data.py

query_data.print_results(r: dict) → None[source]

Print all query results to stdout in a readable format.

query_data.run_queries(database_url: str | None = None) → dict[source]

Execute all analysis queries and return results as a dict.

Parameters:

database_url (str or None) – Optional Postgres connection string. When None, db.py reads DATABASE_URL from the environment. Tests pass in the URL of a throwaway database so production data is never touched.

Returns:

Dict of query results keyed by question name.

Return type:

dict

Data Loading (load_data.py)

load_data.py — Create the applicants table and load Module 2 data into PostgreSQL.

Usage:

python load_data.py

Reads: ../module_2/llm_extend_applicant_data.json Writes: PostgreSQL table applicants in the gradcafe database

Idempotent: uses INSERT … ON CONFLICT (url) DO NOTHING so re-running never creates duplicates (url is treated as the natural unique key for each entry).

load_data.load_data(filepath: str = '/Users/ryangogerty/jhu_software_concepts/module_4/src/../module_2/llm_extend_applicant_data.json', database_url: str | None = None) → int[source]

Load JSON applicant data into PostgreSQL. Returns total row count after insert.

Parameters:

  • filepath (str) – Path to a JSON file containing a list of applicant dicts.

  • database_url (str or None) – Optional Postgres connection string. When None, db.py reads DATABASE_URL from the environment.

Returns:

Total number of rows in the applicants table after loading.

Return type:

int

Scrape + Load Orchestrator (pull_and_load.py)

pull_and_load.py — Scrape new Grad Café entries and add them to the database.

Called by app.py via subprocess when the user clicks “Pull Data”. Scrapes a fresh batch from Grad Café and inserts only new records (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING skips duplicates).

pull_and_load.pull_and_load(scrape_fn=None, save_fn=None, load_fn=None, database_url=None)[source]

Scrape new data and load it into the database.

Parameters are injectable so that tests can pass fake implementations without importing selenium or hitting the network.

Parameters:

  • scrape_fn (callable or None) – Callable matching scrape_data(max_entries=N) signature. Defaults to the real scrape_data from module_2.

  • save_fn (callable or None) – Callable matching save_data(records, filepath) signature. Defaults to the real save_data from module_2.

  • load_fn (callable or None) – Callable matching load_data(filepath, database_url) signature. Defaults to the real load_data from this package.

  • database_url (str or None) – Postgres connection string passed to load_fn.

Returns:

None

Return type:

None
